ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin/Agenda safe-mode reboot preparation + recovery sabotage chain
ucs.description: Detects Qilin (aka Agenda) ransomware's characteristic pre-encryption tradecraft: forcing the host into Safe Mode with Networking via bcdedit to bypass EDR/AV agents that don't load in safe mode, paired within a 1-hour window with shadow-copy/backup deletion. Microsoft (Storm-2406), Trend Micro and Group-IB all flag the bcdedit safe-boot pivot as a Qilin/Agenda hallmark inherited from the group's Rust/Go cross-platform encryptor playbook — the operator must minimise the chance security tooling will block file enumeration before the kernel encryptor runs.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as safebootTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND (Processes.process="*safeboot network*" OR Processes.process="*safeboot minimal*" OR Processes.process="*/set safeboot*") AND NOT (Processes.user="*$" OR Processes.user="SYSTEM") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| rename process as safebootCmd, parent_process_name as parentImg
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as sabotageTime values(Processes.process) as sabotageCmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*shadow*") OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*catalog*") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND (Processes.process="*recoveryenabled no*" OR Processes.process="*bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures*")) by Processes.dest
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
]
| eval deltaSec=abs(safebootTime - sabotageTime)
| where deltaSec < 3600
| eval kc_phase="impact", actor="Qilin/Agenda (Storm-2406)"
| table _time dest user safebootCmd sabotageCmd parentImg deltaSec actor
| sort -_time
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let window = 1h;
let SafeBoot = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("safeboot network", "safeboot minimal", "/set safeboot")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project SafeBootTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              SafeBootCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              SafeBootParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              SafeBootParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let Sabotage = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadow")
         or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("catalog","backup"))
         or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadowcopy" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete")
         or (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("recoveryenabled no","bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures"))
    | project SabotageTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
              SabotageBinary = FileName,
              SabotageCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
SafeBoot
| join kind=inner Sabotage on DeviceId
| where SabotageTime between (SafeBootTime - window .. SafeBootTime + window)
| project SafeBootTime, SabotageTime, DeviceName, AccountName,
          SafeBootCmd, SabotageBinary, SabotageCmd,
          SafeBootParent, SafeBootParentCmd,
          DeltaSec = datetime_diff('second', SabotageTime, SafeBootTime)
| order by SafeBootTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let window = 1h;
let SafeBoot = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\bcdedit.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("safeboot network", "safeboot minimal", "/set safeboot")
    | where Account !endswith "$" and AccountType == "User"
    | project SafeBootTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              SafeBootCmd = CommandLine,
              SafeBootParent = ParentProcessName;
let Sabotage = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where (NewProcessName endswith @"\vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "delete" and CommandLine has "shadow")
         or (NewProcessName endswith @"\wbadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "delete" and CommandLine has_any ("catalog","backup"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith @"\wmic.exe" and CommandLine has "shadowcopy" and CommandLine has "delete")
         or (NewProcessName endswith @"\bcdedit.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("recoveryenabled no","bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures"))
    | project SabotageTime = TimeGenerated, Computer,
              SabotageBinary = NewProcessName,
              SabotageCmd = CommandLine;
SafeBoot
| join kind=inner Sabotage on Computer
| where SabotageTime between (SafeBootTime - window .. SafeBootTime + window)
| project SafeBootTime, SabotageTime, Computer, Account,
          SafeBootCmd, SabotageBinary, SabotageCmd, SafeBootParent,
          DeltaSec = datetime_diff('second', SabotageTime, SafeBootTime)
| order by SafeBootTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Qilin/Agenda Ransomware Safe-Mode Boot Configuration via bcdedit
id: 9a4c1f86-2c7b-4f4e-9b3e-7a4d2f8e1c52
status: experimental
description: Detects bcdedit reconfiguring the boot loader to enter Windows Safe Mode with Networking or Minimal Safe Mode. This is a hallmark Qilin/Agenda ransomware pre-encryption tactic — the operator forces a reboot into safe mode so kernel-mode EDR/AV agents do not load before the Rust/Go encryptor enumerates files.
references:
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/24/h/agenda-ransomware-targets-vmware-esxi-servers.html
  - https://www.group-ib.com/blog/qilin-ransomware/
  - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/operations/threat-actors
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.t1562.009
  - attack.t1529
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\bcdedit.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'safeboot network'
      - 'safeboot minimal'
      - '/set safeboot'
  filter_user:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_img and selection_cmd and not filter_user
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate troubleshooting by a desktop-support engineer (rare; usually paired with /deletevalue, not /set)
level: high
ucs.rationale: The safe-mode-then-reboot pattern is specifically called out by Trend Micro, Microsoft Threat Intel (Storm-2406) and Group-IB as Qilin/Agenda affiliate tradecraft — far more characteristic of Qilin than the generic vssadmin sweep most ransomware crews share. The bcdedit safe-boot toggle exists in Phobos derivatives (e.g. 8Base) too but Qilin uniquely pairs it with their cross-platform Rust encrypt

ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin pre-encryption mass termination of backup/AV/EDR services
ucs.description: Catches Qilin/Agenda's signature defense-evasion burst: an interactive (non-machine) account issuing 8+ service-stop / process-kill commands within 5 minutes against backup, database, hypervisor, and security-product service names. Qilin's leaked builder configures a hard-coded kill-list (Veeam, MSSQL, Exchange, Sophos, SentinelOne, CrowdStrike, Carbon Black, Defender, Acronis, etc.) that the operator triggers manually via net stop / sc stop / taskkill / PowerShell Stop-Service before launching the encryptor — Group-IB published the exact service list extracted from the Agenda Rust binary.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as cmds dc(Processes.process) as uniqueCmds values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="net.exe" OR Processes.process_name="net1.exe" OR Processes.process_name="sc.exe" OR Processes.process_name="taskkill.exe" OR Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe" OR Processes.process_name="wmic.exe") AND (Processes.process="*stop*" OR Processes.process="*Stop-Service*" OR Processes.process="*kill*" OR Processes.process="*delete*") AND (Processes.process="*veeam*" OR Processes.process="*backup*" OR Processes.process="*acronis*" OR Processes.process="*MSSQL*" OR Processes.process="*exchange*" OR Processes.process="*sophos*" OR Processes.process="*defender*" OR Processes.process="*sentinel*" OR Processes.process="*crowdstrike*" OR Processes.process="*carbonblack*" OR Processes.process="*csfalcon*" OR Processes.process="*symantec*" OR Processes.process="*mcafee*" OR Processes.process="*trendmicro*" OR Processes.process="*kaspersky*" OR Processes.process="*eset*" OR Processes.process="*MsMpEng*" OR Processes.process="*WinDefend*") AND NOT (Processes.user="*$" OR Processes.user="SYSTEM" OR Processes.user="LOCAL SERVICE" OR Processes.user="NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=5m
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where count >= 8 AND uniqueCmds >= 5
| eval actor="Qilin/Agenda (Storm-2406)", kc_phase="impact"
| table _time dest user count uniqueCmds parents cmds actor
| sort -_time
ucs.kql:
let window = 5m;
let svc_keywords = dynamic(["veeam","backup","acronis","mssql","sqlserver","exchange","sophos","defender","sentinel","crowdstrike","carbonblack","csfalcon","symantec","mcafee","trendmicro","kaspersky","eset","cylance","msmpeng","windefend","vmware","vmms","vssvc"]);
let stop_verbs = dynamic(["stop ","stop-service","taskkill","sc.exe delete","sc delete"," kill "]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe","sc.exe","taskkill.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| extend cmdLower = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where cmdLower has_any (stop_verbs)
| where cmdLower has_any (svc_keywords)
| summarize TerminationCount = count(),
            DistinctCmds   = dcount(ProcessCommandLine),
            DistinctBins   = dcount(FileName),
            FirstSeen      = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen       = max(Timestamp),
            SampleCmds     = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25),
            Parents        = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
            ParentCmds     = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 10)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, window)
| where TerminationCount >= 8 and DistinctCmds >= 5
| extend Actor = "Qilin/Agenda (Storm-2406)"
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let window = 5m;
let svc_keywords = dynamic(["veeam","backup","acronis","mssql","sqlserver","exchange","sophos","defender","sentinel","crowdstrike","carbonblack","csfalcon","symantec","mcafee","trendmicro","kaspersky","eset","cylance","msmpeng","windefend","vmware","vmms","vssvc"]);
let stop_verbs = dynamic(["stop ","stop-service","taskkill","sc.exe delete","sc delete"," kill "]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName has_any (@"\net.exe",@"\net1.exe",@"\sc.exe",@"\taskkill.exe",@"\powershell.exe",@"\pwsh.exe",@"\wmic.exe")
| where Account !endswith "$" and AccountType == "User"
| where SubjectUserName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| extend cmdLower = tolower(CommandLine)
| where cmdLower has_any (stop_verbs)
| where cmdLower has_any (svc_keywords)
| summarize TerminationCount = count(),
            DistinctCmds   = dcount(CommandLine),
            DistinctBins   = dcount(NewProcessName),
            FirstSeen      = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen       = max(TimeGenerated),
            SampleCmds     = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            Parents        = make_set(ParentProcessName, 10)
            by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, window)
| where TerminationCount >= 8 and DistinctCmds >= 5
| extend Actor = "Qilin/Agenda (Storm-2406)"
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Group-IB's reverse-engineering of the leaked Agenda Rust builder published the exact hard-coded kill-list embedded in Qilin's encryptor — Veeam/MSSQL/Acronis on the backup/DB side and Sophos/SentinelOne/CrowdStrike/Defender on the security side. The detection isn't 'kill a service' (every admin does that) — it's 'an interactive user account stops 8+ services across 5+ distinct command lines spanni
